title: claytonia spathulata var. exigua
intro: Claytonia spathulata var. exigua, also known as the narrow-leaved miner's lettuce or narrow-leaved winter purslane, is a perennial herb native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is also valued for its nutritional benefits, including its high content of vitamins A, C, and K. The health benefits of Claytonia spathulata var. exigua include reducing inflammation, supporting immune function, and promoting healthy digestion. The leaves and stems of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and nutritional benefits.
